macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: PCE Inflation at 2.80% Year-over-Year, Personal Income Declines
items.summary: The Joint Economic Committee reported that headline PCE inflation reached 2.80% year-over-year in February, while personal income decreased 0.07%. Real PCE increased modestly by 0.10%, indicating slowing consumer spending.

items.title: Q4 2025 Real GDP Growth Revised to 4.24% Annualized
items.summary: The Joint Economic Committee's third estimate for Q4 2025 showed real GDP growth increased to 4.24% annualized, with nominal GDP reaching $31.423 trillion.

items.title: Fed Chair Powell to Deliver Speech; Multiple Economic Data Releases Scheduled
items.summary: Fed Chair Powell is scheduled to speak, with multiple key economic indicators being released including Initial Jobless Claims (230K), ISM Manufacturing PMI (52.4), and Chicago PMI (52.8).

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $72,000 on U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Announcement
items.summary: Bitcoin rose approximately 4% to over $72,000 and Ether about 6% following President Trump’s U.S.-Iran two-week ceasefire announcement, driven by new long positions rather than short squeezes, with open interest surging over $2 billion for both assets.[1][2][5]

items.title: CryptoQuant Reports U.S. Investor Demand Rebounds Amid Rally
items.summary: Coinbase Premium Index turned positive for Bitcoin and Ether after weeks of negativity, signaling recovering U.S. demand; analysts eye $79,000 as next Bitcoin target if ceasefire holds.[1]

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Exceed $300 Million in 24 Hours
items.summary: Over $300 million in futures liquidations occurred, primarily longs totaling ~$301 million, amid market volatility; separate $111 million event noted earlier but outside 24-hour window.[3][5]

items.title: Mixed Crypto Price Action as Market Cap Hits $2.52 Trillion
items.summary: Total crypto market cap rose 1.4% to $2.52 trillion; Bitcoin up 0.8% to $72,271, XRP steady at $1.35, Solana down 0.5% to $83.98, while Fear & Greed Index remains at Extreme Fear (14).[2]

items.title: US SEC Labels Past Crypto Prosecutions Under Gensler as Misguided
items.summary: SEC describes its previous practice of prosecuting 95 crypto companies since 2022 under former chairman Gary Gensler as misguided.[4]

items.title: Split Capital Hedge Fund Shuts Down Citing Declining Crypto Project Quality
items.summary: Zahir Abtikar announced closure of Split Capital, arguing the hedge fund model is ineffective for crypto due to fewer high-quality projects and prevalence of imitators.[4]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Meta Releases Muse Spark AI Model with Impressive Benchmarks
items.summary: Meta has launched Muse Spark, a new AI model excelling in benchmarks against top models like Google Gemini and Opus, featuring advanced multimodal RAG and multi-agent orchestration capabilities.
items.source: YouTube - Muse Spark: Meta's NEW AI Just Went Next Level

items.title: Anthropic Surpasses OpenAI in Revenue at $30 Billion Run Rate
items.summary: Anthropic has reportedly achieved a $30 billion revenue run rate, surpassing OpenAI's $2 billion, with lower training costs giving investors a better deal.
items.source: YouTube - Mercor Hack & Why Now is the Time for Cyber

items.title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Managed Agents for Automation
items.summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Managed Agents, a zero-infrastructure solution for automation that could rival tools like n8n, though it faces challenges like missing cron features and high API costs.
items.source: YouTube - Will Anthropic's New Move Change the Automation World?

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: FaZe Clan Faces Major Roster Changes After CS Major Exit
items.summary: FaZe's karrigan expresses willingness to be dropped from the team amid poor performance, with speculation about Woxic joining and potential MOUZ moves for Magisk. G2's Hunter will miss IEM Rio due to a foot injury.

items.title: Mongil: Star Dive Gacha Game Set for April 15 Release
items.summary: Upcoming gacha game Mongil: Star Dive is scheduled for release on April 15, 2026, with pre-registration available. Gameplay impressions shared after 20+ hours of playtesting.

items.title: Dice Tower News Covers Latest Board Game Announcements
items.summary: New zombie side expansion for Game Found via crowdfunding, a new Sashi game, and Incredible Dreams' upcoming title highlighted in board gaming updates.

items.title: NBA 2K26 Competitive Stage Features Intense Trash Talk Matches
items.summary: A 2K pro player takes on trash talkers in NBA 2K26's competitive stage court, showcasing high-level gameplay and builds.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Secures Google Commitment for Future Xeon Processors in Data Centers
items.summary: Alphabet's Google has agreed to a multiyear deal to use upcoming generations of Intel's Xeon processors and customized IPUs for networking, security, and storage in data centers. Intel's stock rose up to 3.6% following the announcement, supporting its push into AI infrastructure spending.[1]

items.title: Google's Vulnerability Reward Program Pays Record $17 Million to Security Researchers
items.summary: Google awarded a record $17 million through its Vulnerability Reward Program last year, up 40% from 2024, to over 700 external security researchers. This highlights ongoing efforts to enhance cybersecurity across its products.[5]
